assertions.title: Long tool output remains inspectable without collapsing the shell

steps.action: Launch the real TUI and observe the shell before transcript overflow.
steps.expected: Header, panels, transcript, composer, and status should all be visible before interacting with long output.
steps.observed: The initial shell rendered with all primary landmarks intact in the standard 120x36 viewport.

steps.action: Drive the transcript into a large live-output state and capture it while the shell is still waiting on the turn.
steps.expected: Large command output should stay inside the chat viewport without collapsing adjacent shell chrome.
steps.observed: The chat panel showed multi-line command output from long-running tool activity while the header, issue desk, composer, and status bar all remained visible.

steps.action: Scroll upward with PageUp and Shift+Up to inspect earlier output in the same session.
steps.expected: Earlier output should remain inspectable and the shell chrome should stay intact.
steps.observed: Scrolling exposed earlier tool-output lines and a completed command block while the surrounding shell chrome stayed intact and readable.

assertions.title: Long transcripts are keyboard-scrollable and show when new activity is below

steps.action: Move off the live bottom with PageUp and Shift+Up while a real turn is still active.
steps.expected: The transcript should keep the reading position stable and show a visible newer-activity indicator below.
steps.observed: The TUI showed `▲ 1507 earlier lines above` and `▼ 149 newer lines below — PageDown or Shift+↓ to follow live` while preserving the scrolled reading position.

steps.action: Stay off the live bottom while the turn finalizes into history.
steps.expected: The operator should remain anchored on the same lines and the newer-activity indicator should continue reflecting activity below.
steps.observed: After the turn finalized, the same scrolled view remained stable and the indicator updated to `▼ 176 newer lines below`, confirming that newer activity accumulated below without snapping the viewport.

steps.action: Return to the live bottom with PageDown.
steps.expected: The operator should be able to reach live bottom and clear the newer-activity indicator.
steps.observed: Repeated PageDown presses moved toward the live bottom, but within the very large transcript and tuistory runtime limits I did not capture a same-run bottom state with the indicator fully cleared.

assertions.issues: Blocked: the real TUI clearly supported off-bottom keyboard scrolling and a live newer-activity indicator, but I could not conclusively capture the same long run returning all the way to live bottom before tuistory timeouts.

assertions.title: Layout stays within the viewport through send, stream, and resize

steps.expected: Header, management panels, transcript, composer, and status should all fit in the viewport.
steps.observed: The initial shell rendered all primary landmarks cleanly inside the viewport.

steps.action: Observe the shell while a real turn is active and the transcript is carrying large output.
steps.expected: Active streaming should remain within the viewport without dropping shell chrome.
steps.observed: During the live waiting/streaming state, the transcript carried large output while the header, panels, composer, and status bar all remained visible.

steps.action: Observe the same shell after the turn completes.
steps.expected: The completed state should remain inside the viewport and preserve the same shell landmarks.
steps.observed: After completion the shell returned to an idle composer without losing the header, side panels, chat frame, or status bar.

steps.action: Resize into narrower stacked layouts at 74x24 and 68x20.
steps.expected: The UI should reflow compactly while preserving brand/header, panels, transcript/composer, and status landmarks.
steps.observed: Both narrow sizes reflowed into compact layouts; the branding/header, management areas, chat/composer, and compact status line remained visible within the terminal viewport.

frictions.description: `tuistory type` was unreliable in this Ink composer and long typing attempts could destabilize the session.
frictions.resolution: Switched to explicit per-key `tuistory press` input with send fallbacks (`Enter`, `Return`, then `Ctrl+M`).

frictions.description: Some longer natural-language prompts were interpreted as issue-creation requests rather than direct shell-tool runs.
frictions.resolution: Used the simpler working prompt form `say before then run command ... then say after` and relied on the real session's existing long tool-output transcript where needed.

blockers.description: The very large live transcript plus tuistory runtime limits prevented a conclusive same-run capture of the indicator-cleared live-bottom state after repeated PageDown attempts.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Repeated PageDown navigation across the long transcript and additional relaunches to isolate the return-to-bottom check, but the captured same-run evidence still stopped short of a fully cleared indicator state.

summary: Tested 3 assertions through the real TUI. Passed: VAL-CHAT-004 and VAL-POLISH-001. Blocked: VAL-CHAT-005 because off-bottom scrolling and newer-activity indication were observed, but a same-run return-to-live-bottom capture remained inconclusive before tuistory limits were hit.
